description_long: This object is a delicate, stylized flower with a slender, slightly curved green stem and a single bloom at the top. The flower head is composed of loosely arranged, overlapping pink petals that form a somewhat spiral pattern, giving it a soft, organic appearance. Two leaves are attached to the stem, one higher and one lower, both with a lanceolate shape and subtle veining. The petals are thin and wispy, with some transparency, suggesting a lightweight and fragile structure. The overall form is airy and minimalistic, with visible gaps between the petals and leaves. The coloration is primarily pink for the petals and green for the stem and leaves, with hints of white and gray in the highlights and shadows.
description: A delicate pink flower with a slender green stem and two lanceolate leaves, featuring loosely arranged, spiral petals. The design is airy, minimalistic, and visually lightweight.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a side angle, showing the curved stem, two leaves, and the spiral arrangement of the pink petals.
description_view_1: This view presents the flower from a slightly different side, emphasizing the vertical alignment of the stem and the layered petals.
description_view_2: The flower is viewed more frontally, highlighting the central spiral of the petals and the prominent upper leaf.
description_view_3: A rear or alternate side view, showing the back of the flower head and the positioning of both leaves along the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a loose, spiral configuration with overlapping and layering, lacking strict radial symmetry. The arrangement is somewhat irregular, with petals fanning out from the center in a whorled, spiral pattern.
